e5ca8251.title: Delivery Executive and Architect, Google Cloud
e5ca8251.description:
**Minimum qualifications:**
* Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience.
* 7 years of experience in program management, client services or management consulting.
* Experience delivering enterprise digital transformation projects to customers. (e.g., cloud migrations or multi\-cloud transformations across AI, data warehousing and analytics, application development, or cyber security).
* Ability to travel up to 50% of the time as required.


**Preferred qualifications:**
* Experience leading cloud\-based transformation programs and software development solutions.
* Experience working with new and emerging technologies, methodologies, and solutions in the cloud and broader enterprise technology transformation space.
* Experience collaborating with channel partners, systems integrators, and third\-party developers to deliver high\-impact solutions.
* Experience with programs pertaining to data and analytics systems and implementations.


**About The Job**
 As a Technical Solutions Consultant, you will be responsible for the technical relationship of our largest advertising clients and/or product partners. You will lead cross\-functional teams in Engineering, Sales and Product Management to leverage emerging technologies for our external clients/partners. From concept design and testing to data analysis and support, you will oversee the technical execution and business operations of Google's online advertising platforms and/or product partnerships.
   

  

 You will be able to balance business and partner needs with technical constraints, develop innovative, cutting edge solutions and act as a partner and consultant to those you are working with. You will also be able to build tools and automate products, oversee the technical execution and business operations of Google's partnerships, as well as develop product strategy and prioritize projects and resources.
   

  

 The Strategic Partnerships, Delivery and Innovation team in Google Cloud have two priorities \- Engineering Incubation and Customer Incubation. We are involved in engineering experimentation and customer innovation using the latest AI products to help customers solve problems with Generative AI. In this role, you will work very closely with our product and engineering teams.
   

  

 Google Cloud accelerates every organization’s ability to digitally transform its business and industry. We deliver enterprise\-grade solutions that leverage Google’s cutting\-edge technology, and tools that help developers build more sustainably. Customers in more than 200 countries and territories turn to Google Cloud as their trusted partner to enable growth and solve their most critical business problems.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Lead enterprise\-wide Google Cloud adoption, workload prioritization, and cultural readiness while holding accountability at the board and executive level to ensure the program realizes financial benefits and business objectives.
* Define the overall governance model and service delivery plan, establishing a steady cadence to manage the ongoing execution of multi\-functional, large\-scale cloud transformation projects.
* Oversee the entire presales scoping life\-cycle and customer requirements, tracking timelines, managing stakeholder communication, and building risk mitigation plans.
* Deliver technical leadership for enterprise cloud architecture, documenting migration paths, integration strategies, and complete solution designs using Google Cloud best practices.
* Serve as the single point of technical contact for stakeholders into the consulting delivery team while capturing and sharing best practices to scale expertise.


 Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

000a1490.title: Senior Engineer I, Cloud Operations and Infrastructure
000a1490.description:
Join us as a Senior Engineer focused on Cloud Operations and Infrastructure. You'll design and manage secure, scalable cloud environments that power innovation—collaborating across teams to deliver high\-performing, cost\-optimized solutions.
   

  

**What will you do?**
* Build and maintain secure, scalable cloud infrastructure on leading platforms
* Automate infrastructure deployment and configuration using industry\-standard tools
* Monitor and optimize system performance, availability, and cloud spend
* Partner with development, operations, and security teams to ensure seamless cloud operations


**What are the key skills we are looking for?**
* Cloud Platforms — intermediate level; building and managing environments on AWS, Azure, or GCP
* Infrastructure as Code — intermediate level; automating provisioning with Terraform or similar tools
* Containerization and Orchestration — intermediate level; deploying and managing workloads using Kubernetes
* Cloud Networking — intermediate level; designing secure, high\-performance network architectures
* Continuous Integration — intermediate level; implementing CI pipelines to streamline deployments
* Cloud Cost Management — intermediate level; monitoring and optimizing spend across cloud resources
* Cybersecurity — intermediate level; applying security best practices to protect cloud infrastructure
* Incident Management — intermediate level; responding to and resolving operational issues efficiently


**What qualifications will make you successful?**
* Strong problem\-solving skills and the ability to troubleshoot complex technical challenges
* Excellent collaboration and communication skills across technical and non\-technical teams
* Critical thinking mindset with a focus on continuous improvement and innovation
* Ability to work independently while mentoring and guiding peers on technical initiatives


**What's in it for me?**
* Opportunity to work with cutting\-edge cloud technologies and drive meaningful impact
* Collaborative team culture that values innovation and technical excellence
* Career growth through mentorship, training, and exposure to complex infrastructure challenges
* Flexible work options designed to support your work\-life balance


 Bring your cloud expertise to a team that's ready to welcome you—apply today!
   

  

 Rewards designed for you
   

  

 Our Total Rewards is our way of saying: We see you and we value you. It’s more than just pay and benefits—it’s a meaningful investment in you. It is designed to help you perform, grow, feel safe, and elevate your potential. The package helps you care for yourself and your family, plan your future, grow your skills and career, collaborate in an inclusive workplace, and contribute to your community. At Schneider Electric, we’re here for what matters most to you. Discover more at our Career Page.
   

  

* Country\-specific programs and initiatives may be available.


**Looking to make an IMPACT with your career?**
 When you are thinking about joining a new team, culture matters. At Schneider Electric, our values and behaviors are the foundation for creating a great culture to support business success. We believe that our IMPACT values – Inclusion, Mastery, Purpose, Action, Curiosity, Teamwork – starts with us.
   

  

 IMPACT is also your invitation to join Schneider Electric where you can contribute to turning sustainability ambition into actions, no matter what role you play. It is a call to connect your career with the ambition of achieving a more resilient, efficient, and sustainable world.
   

  

 We are looking for IMPACT Makers; exceptional people who turn sustainability ambitions into actions at the intersection of automation, electrification, and digitization. We celebrate IMPACT Makers and believe everyone has the potential to be one.
   

  

 Become an IMPACT Maker with Schneider Electric – apply today!
   

  

 €40 billion global revenue
   

  

 \+9% organic growth
   

  

 150 000\+ employees in 100\+ countries
   

  

 You must submit an online application to be considered for any position with us. This position will be posted until filled.
   

  

*Schneider Electric aspires to be the most inclusive and caring company in the world, by providing equitable opportunities to everyone, everywhere, and ensuring all employees feel uniquely valued and safe to contribute their best. We mirror the diversity of the communities in which we operate, and ‘inclusion’ is one of our core values. We believe our differences make us stronger as a company and as individuals and we are committed to championing inclusivity in everything we do.*
*At Schneider Electric, we uphold the highest standards of ethics and compliance, and we believe that trust is a foundational value. Our Trust Charter is our Code of Conduct and demonstrates our commitment to ethics, safety, sustainability, quality and cybersecurity, underpinning every aspect of our business and our willingness to behave and respond respectfully and in good faith to all our stakeholders. You can find out more about our Trust Charter here*
*Schneider Electric is an Equal Opportunity Employer. It is our policy to provide equal employment and advancement opportunities in the areas of recruiting, hiring, training, transferring, and promoting all qualified individuals regardless of race, religion, color, gender, disability, national origin, ancestry, age, military status, sexual orientation, marital status, or any other legally protected characteristic or conduct.*

a85db360.title: Consulting-HC-IIO-HC Forward-Cloud architect
a85db360.description:
**Human Capital**

The Human Capital Offering Portfolio focuses on helping organizations manage and sustain their performance through their most important asset: their people. Centered on five core issues, this Portfolio signifies to the market that we see Human Capital as a topic critical to the C\-Suite. As we go\-to\-market, we will show our clients that we serve more than HR organizations – from the CEO to CFO, Risk Manager to Business Unit leader—and that we deliver on our issues and help create value for our clients.


**Insights, Innovation \& Operate Offering:**
=============================================

Our Insights, Innovation \& Operate Offering is designed\[LR1] to enhance key aspects of our clients' businesses by leveraging cutting\-edge technology, data, and a blend of deep technical and human expertise. We innovate and deliver creative, industry\-specific solutions that streamline operations and accelerate speed\-to\-value.

**Position Summary**

**Cloud Solution Architect (Senior Consultants, Managers)**

 **Role Summary**


We are seeking a seasoned Cloud Solution Architect to lead end\-to\-end architecture for cloud\-native web applications across diverse technology stacks. The role is deliberately **stack\-agnostic**: architecture judgment, patterns, and multi\-cloud fluency matter more than commitment to any single language or framework. Candidates may come from MEAN/MERN (Node.js/TypeScript), Python (FastAPI/Flask/Django), Java (Spring Boot), .NET, or other modern ecosystems. You will shape target\-state architectures, guide engineering teams, and ensure secure, scalable deployments across multi\-cloud environments (AWS/Azure/GCP). You will partner with product, security, platform, AI, and data teams to deliver resilient solutions using modern DevSecOps, containerization, and infrastructure automation.

**Experience bands:**

* **Senior Consultant**: 8\+ years total experience in software engineering and architecture
* **Manager**: 12\+ years total experience in software engineering and architecture

**Key Responsibilities**

* Lead solution architecture for large\-scale web applications across diverse stacks (MEAN/MERN, Python, Java, .NET, or equivalent), including modernization and greenfield builds.
* Define cloud reference architectures and implementation patterns for multi\-cloud deployments (AWS/Azure/GCP), balancing portability with platform\-native optimizations.
* Design and govern API strategies (REST/GraphQL), integration patterns, event\-driven architectures, and microservices/service decomposition.
* Architect scalable data solutions using any of: managed relational (AWS RDS/Aurora, Azure Database for PostgreSQL/MySQL), managed NoSQL (AWS DynamoDB), document stores (MongoDB), managed analytics/warehouse (AWS Redshift), and in\-memory cache (AWS ElastiCache, Azure Redis Cache) — alongside messaging/streaming and search.
* Establish non\-functional requirements and ensure performance, reliability, scalability, observability, and cost efficiency (FinOps) are built in.
* Drive cloud security architecture: IAM, secrets management, encryption, network segmentation, WAF, threat modeling, and compliance\-aligned controls.
* Build and influence CI/CD and DevSecOps practices: automated testing, SAST/DAST, artifact management, policy\-as\-code, and release governance.
* Define infrastructure\-as\-code and deployment automation (e.g., Terraform/CloudFormation/Bicep), and standardize environment provisioning.
* Guide container and platform strategy (Docker, Kubernetes/EKS/AKS/GKE, ingress/service mesh as appropriate) and operational runbooks.
* **For cloud\-deployed AI application platforms**, own the cloud platform and infrastructure architecture (networking, scaling, security, CI/CD, IaC, observability) in joint partnership with the AI Solutions Architect, who owns the AI application layer (agent orchestration, LLM integration, RAG, guardrails).
* **For cloud\-deployed data platforms** (lakehouses/delta lakes, data hubs, streaming architectures), own the cloud platform and infrastructure architecture in joint partnership with the Data Integration Architect, who owns the data architecture layer (pipelines, lakehouse/delta lake design, governance, ETL/ELT).
* Collaborate with stakeholders to translate business requirements into architecture decisions; produce clear artifacts (HLD/LLD, ADRs, roadmaps).
* Mentor engineers and architects; conduct design reviews and enforce architecture standards and best practices.
* Support production readiness, incident response improvements, SRE\-style reliability practices, and continuous optimization.

**Required Qualifications**

* 8\+ years (Senior Consultant) or 12\+ years (Manager) total experience in software engineering and architecture.
* **Demonstrated architecture experience across multiple web application stacks** — any two or more of MEAN/MERN, Python (FastAPI/Flask/Django), Java (Spring Boot), .NET, or equivalent. Deep specialization in one is expected, working fluency in at least one additional stack is required.
* **Strong experience with managed database services across multiple paradigms**, with demonstrated breadth across the HCF ecosystem – **any of** (**deep experience in at least one paradigm** is expected; working familiarity across most is required):
* + Managed relational: AWS RDS/Aurora (PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQL Server, MariaDB, Oracle), Azure Database for PostgreSQL/MySQL
	+ Document: MongoDB
	+ Managed NoSQL key\-value: AWS DynamoDB
	+ Managed analytics/warehouse: AWS Redshift
	+ In\-memory cache: AWS ElastiCache, Azure Redis Cache
* **Cross\-functional fluency with AI/ML and data architecture patterns** — working familiarity with LLM application deployment (model gateways, vector DBs such as pgvector, RAG pipelines), data platform concepts (lakehouse, ETL/ELT, streaming), and the infrastructure requirements they impose on cloud architecture. Not expected to own these domains, but must design cloud environments that host them and collaborate with AI Solutions and Data Integration architects in joint reviews.
* **Hands\-on proficiency with AI coding assistants** (Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Cursor, Windsurf or equivalent) for architecture work, design documentation, and engineering workflows. Familiarity **with agentic engineering** patterns is expected.
* Strong experience designing and operating MongoDB solutions (data modeling, indexing, replication, sharding, performance tuning)
* Proven multi\-cloud deployment experience across AWS/Azure/GCP (at least two strongly preferred)
* Strong understanding of cloud networking and security fundamentals (VPC/VNet, subnets, routing, DNS, load balancing, TLS, IAM)
* Hands\-on experience with containers and Kubernetes and cloud\-managed services (EKS/AKS/GKE)
* CI/CD expertise (e.g., GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins, Azure DevOps) and IaC (Terraform strongly preferred)
* Observability experience (logging/metrics/tracing) using tools such as CloudWatch/Azure Monitor/GCP Operations, Prometheus/Grafana, OpenTelemetry, ELK
* Strong architecture documentation and communication skills; ability to influence technical direction across teams

**Preferred Qualifications**

* Cloud certifications (one or more): AWS Solutions Architect (Professional), Azure Solutions Architect Expert, Google Professional Cloud Architect
* Experience with service mesh (Istio/Linkerd), API gateways (Kong/Apigee/AWS API Gateway/Azure APIM), and eventing (Kafka, Pub/Sub, Event Hubs)
* Experience with zero\-trust patterns, policy\-as\-code (OPA/Gatekeeper), and security tooling (Vault, Prisma, Wiz, etc.)
* Experience with multi\-region/high\-availability designs and disaster recovery planning (RTO/RPO)
* Familiarity with platform engineering and internal developer platforms (Backstage, golden paths, reusable templates)
* Domain experience in regulated environments (financial services, healthcare, public sector)

**Key Competencies**

* Architecture leadership and pragmatic decision\-making (trade\-offs, risk management, technology selection)
* Strong stakeholder management; ability to translate ambiguity into executable plans
* Hands\-on builder mindset—comfortable diving into code, pipelines, and cloud consoles when needed
* Coaching/mentoring and quality\-driven engineering culture

**Education**

* Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field (or equivalent practical experience). Master’s degree is a plus
**Our purpose**  


Deloitte’s purpose is to make an impact that matters for our people, clients, and communities. At Deloitte, purpose is synonymous with how we work every day. It defines who we are. Our purpose comes through in our work with clients that enables impact and value in their organizations, as well as through our own investments, commitments, and actions across areas that help drive positive outcomes for our communities.
**Our people and culture**  


Our inclusive culture empowers our people to be who they are, contribute their unique perspectives, and make a difference individually and collectively. It enables us to leverage different ways of thinking, ideas and perspectives, and bring more creativity and innovation to help solve our clients’ most complex challenges. This makes Deloitte one of the most rewarding places to work.
**Professional development**  


At Deloitte, professionals have the opportunity to work with some of the best and discover what works best for them. Here, we prioritize professional growth, offering diverse learning and networking opportunities to help accelerate careers and enhance leadership skills. Our state\-of\-the\-art DU: The Leadership Center in India, located in Hyderabad, represents a tangible symbol of our commitment to the holistic growth and development of our people. Explore DU: The Leadership Center in India.
**Benefits to help you thrive**  


At Deloitte, we know that great people make a great organization. Our comprehensive rewards program helps us deliver a distinctly Deloitte experience that helps that empowers our professionals to thrive mentally, physically, and financially—and live their purpose. To support our professionals and their loved ones, we offer a broad range of benefits. Eligibility requirements may be based on role, tenure, type of employment and/ or other criteria. Learn more about what working at Deloitte can mean for you.
**Recruiting tips**  


From developing a stand out resume to putting your best foot forward in the interview, we want you to feel prepared and confident as you explore opportunities at Deloitte. Check out recruiting tips from Deloitte recruiters.

52f41eb3.title: Presales Solution Architect – Digital Workplace Services
52f41eb3.description:
**Role Description**
 Job Description
   

  

 Position
   

  

 Presales Solution Architect – Digital Workplace Services
   

  

**Job Summary**
 The Presales Solution Architect for Digital Workplace Services will play a critical role in shaping technology and managed service solutions that enable modern, productive and user\-centric workplace environments.
   

  

 The role will partner closely with sales, practice, delivery, engineering, commercial teams, technology partners and clients to understand business requirements, develop tailored solutions and articulate the value of Digital Workplace Services. The solution scope may include Service Desk, Field Services, Modern Endpoint Management, Device Lifecycle Services, Microsoft 365 and Collaboration, Digital Employee Experience, Workplace AI and Automation.
   

  

 The ideal candidate will be a Digital Workplace generalist with broad solutioning experience across multiple workplace service areas and deeper expertise in one or more domains. The candidate should combine technical understanding, managed services solutioning, commercial awareness, strong documentation capability and confident client\-facing communication skills.
   

  

 Key Responsibilities
   

  

 Solution Design and Architecture
   

  

* Lead the design of end\-to\-end Digital Workplace solutions spanning Service Desk, Field Services, VIP support, endpoint management, device lifecycle services, Microsoft 365, collaboration platforms, Digital Employee Experience, digital adoption and workplace automation.
* Translate customer business, operational and technical requirements into scalable, supportable and cost\-effective solution architectures.
* Design integrated operating models covering service scope, support tiers, delivery locations, coverage windows, staffing, governance, service levels and enabling technology platforms.
* Coordinate inputs from domain specialists, delivery teams, technology vendors and service partners to create a cohesive end\-to\-end solution.
* Develop solution blueprints, architecture diagrams, service descriptions, service catalogues, operating models and transformation roadmaps.
* Define solution assumptions, dependencies, exclusions, risks, customer responsibilities and third\-party responsibilities.
* Ensure proposed solutions are aligned with customer objectives, operational requirements and delivery capabilities.


 Presales and Bid Support
   

  

* Work with sales and account teams to qualify opportunities and define appropriate Digital Workplace solution strategies.
* Lead or contribute to RFP, RFI and proposal responses.
* Conduct customer discovery workshops, requirements sessions, assessments and solution walkthroughs.
* Develop high\-quality solution proposals, response documents, PowerPoint presentations, bid\-orals decks, executive narratives and supporting solution artefacts.
* Build value propositions, business cases, ROI and TCO models, transformation benefits and competitive differentiators.
* Lead or support bid\-orals presentations, solution walkthroughs and client solution\-defence sessions.
* Respond to technical, operational and commercial challenges relating to the proposed solution.
* Support customer due diligence, clarification sessions and commercial negotiations.
* Ensure customer requirements are addressed clearly, consistently and traceably within the proposed solution.


 Managed Services and Commercial Solutioning
   

  

* Develop operating models, resource estimates, staffing structures, delivery\-location strategies and support coverage models.
* Create detailed effort estimates across transition, transformation, engineering, service management and business\-as\-usual operations.
* Define service levels, KPIs, OLAs, XLAs, governance structures and reporting requirements.
* Develop transition, mobilisation, transformation and continuous\-improvement approaches.
* Provide resource models, effort estimates, technology requirements and commercial inputs.
* Work with pricing, finance, delivery and partner teams to validate solution cost, feasibility and competitiveness.
* Support the development of pricing models, including resource\-based, transaction\-based, per\-user, per\-device and outcome\-based constructs where applicable.
* Participate in internal solution reviews, risk reviews and commercial approval processes.
* Ensure proposed commitments are measurable, deliverable and commercially viable.


 Field Services and Partner Solutioning
   

  

* Work with global, regional and local field\-service partners to develop onsite support, scheduled visit and dispatch\-based service models.
* Validate partner coverage, delivery capability, service hours, language support, logistics capability and regional constraints.
* Obtain and validate partner effort, pricing, lead\-time and service\-level inputs.
* Define scope boundaries, hand\-offs, escalation paths, service responsibilities and governance requirements for partner\-delivered services.
* Integrate partner\-delivered field services into the overall Digital Workplace operating model and commercial solution.
* Support partner selection, benchmarking and solution\-risk assessment where required.


 Customer Engagement
   

  

* Present and articulate Digital Workplace capabilities, solution options and transformation roadmaps to senior and executive stakeholders.
* Facilitate customer workshops and consultative discussions across business, technology and operational stakeholders.
* Conduct demonstrations, proof\-of\-concept planning and solution walkthroughs where applicable.
* Serve as a trusted advisor on workplace technologies, managed service models, employee experience and workplace transformation.
* Translate technical and operational concepts into clear business outcomes and measurable value.
* Defend solution assumptions, design decisions, operating models, service levels, effort estimates and commercial constructs in front of customers.


 Collaboration and Delivery Enablement
   

  

* Work closely with service delivery, engineering, transition, operations and practice teams to ensure solution feasibility and accuracy.
* Engage product vendors, OEMs and service partners where third\-party capabilities form part of the solution.
* Develop and review Statements of Work, service descriptions, responsibility matrices, assumptions, dependencies and contractual solution commitments.
* Ensure alignment between the proposed solution, contractual scope, pricing model and delivery approach.
* Provide knowledge transfer and solution guidance to transition and delivery teams.
* Support delivery mobilisation and provide solution continuity during the initial stages of service implementation.
* Ensure that risks, dependencies and unresolved solution decisions are clearly documented and transferred into delivery governance.


 Practice and Capability Development
   

  

* Contribute to the development of reusable solution assets, reference architectures, estimators, service descriptions, accelerators and proposal content.
* Support the standardisation and continuous improvement of Digital Workplace presales methods and solution governance.
* Evaluate emerging workplace technologies and assess their relevance to customer requirements and service offerings.
* Share knowledge, market insights and solution lessons across sales, presales, practice and delivery teams.
* Contribute to the development and improvement of Digital Workplace offerings, service catalogues and commercial models.


 Market and Technology Expertise
   

  

* Maintain awareness of developments across modern endpoint management, Microsoft 365, collaboration, workplace AI, automation, Digital Employee Experience and device lifecycle services.
* Understand market trends, customer priorities, competitor offerings and evolving workplace operating models.
* Assess the impact of new technologies on service delivery, user experience, productivity and support operations.
* Help shape Digital Workplace offerings and transformation propositions based on market and customer requirements.


 Candidate Profile
   

  

* Broad understanding of Digital Workplace technologies, managed services and operating models.
* Ability to understand complex customer environments and translate requirements into coherent and workable solutions.
* Ability to integrate inputs from multiple domain specialists and partners into a single end\-to\-end solution.
* Strong customer\-facing presales, consulting and stakeholder\-management experience.
* Comfortable working in fast\-paced, multi\-stakeholder and deadline\-driven deal environments.
* Strong commercial awareness and understanding of how solution decisions affect cost, risk and delivery.
* Ability to balance customer expectations, technical feasibility, operational practicality and commercial viability.
* Strong storytelling capability with the ability to explain technical and operational concepts in business language.
* Strong document\-creation and presentation capability.
* Ability to work independently while coordinating effectively across sales, practice, delivery, engineering, finance, pricing and partner teams.


**Experience**
* 8–15 years of overall IT industry experience.
* At least 5 years of experience in Digital Workplace presales, solution architecture, consulting or managed services solutioning.
* Proven experience designing solutions across several of the following areas:
+ Service Desk and omnichannel end\-user support
+ Field Services, onsite support and VIP support
+ Modern Endpoint Management using Microsoft Intune, Configuration Manager, Workspace ONE, Ivanti or comparable platforms
+ Windows 10 and Windows 11 lifecycle management
+ Device provisioning using Windows Autopilot, imaging, migration and deployment methods
+ Device lifecycle management, asset services, logistics, refresh, recovery and disposal
+ Microsoft 365, Teams and workplace collaboration services
+ Digital Employee Experience and proactive experience management
+ Workplace AI, conversational AI, self\-service and workflow automation
+ Knowledge management, digital adoption and user enablement

* Experience working with field\-service partners, OEMs and regional service providers.
* Experience designing managed service operating models, support structures, resource models and coverage approaches.
* Experience defining SLAs, KPIs, XLAs, governance, reporting and continuous\-improvement frameworks.
* Experience supporting RFPs, proposals, customer workshops, bid\-orals presentations and solution\-defence sessions.
* Experience creating effort estimates, staffing models, commercial inputs, transition approaches and transformation roadmaps.
* Experience developing and reviewing Statements of Work and contractual service descriptions.
* Experience working with globally distributed customers, delivery teams, technology vendors and service partners is preferred.


 Technical and Service Skills
   

  

* Strong understanding of enterprise endpoint architectures and modern device\-management platforms.
* Strong understanding of Service Desk, Field Services and managed workplace support models.
* Understanding of device lifecycle processes, including procurement, provisioning, deployment, support, refresh, recovery and disposal.
* Working knowledge of Microsoft 365, Microsoft Teams and enterprise collaboration platforms.
* Familiarity with Digital Employee Experience platforms, experience measurement, telemetry, proactive remediation and employee\-experience improvement.
* Understanding of workplace AI, conversational AI, automation and self\-service use cases.
* Familiarity with ITIL processes, IT service management, governance and service\-delivery models.
* Ability to design support\-tier structures, coverage models, service levels and operational processes.
* Ability to develop resource estimates, effort models and solution\-level commercial inputs.
* Knowledge of PowerShell, Microsoft Graph API or other automation technologies is an advantage.
* Ability to create high\-quality architecture documents, solution proposals, PowerPoint presentations, bid\-orals decks, operating models and Statements of Work.


 Soft Skills
   

  

* Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
* Strong presentation and storytelling capability for customer and executive audiences.
* Strong analytical, problem\-solving and consultative skills.
* Ability to manage ambiguity and develop solutions from incomplete or evolving requirements.
* Strong attention to detail and commitment to solution quality.
* Ability to manage multiple opportunities and competing priorities.
* Collaborative mindset with strong stakeholder\-management and influencing skills.
* Ability to challenge assumptions constructively and identify solution risks.
* Strong ownership, accountability and ability to work independently.
* Confidence in presenting and defending solutions before customers and internal governance teams.

 cloud infrastructure,infrastructure as code,virtual private cloud,cloud security,

ffd0d420.title: IT manager, enterprise infrastructure services & cloud
ffd0d420.description:
Colt provides network, voice and data centre services to thousands of businesses around the world, allowing them to focus on delivering their business goals instead of the underlying infrastructure.
   

  

**Why we need this role**
 The Manager IT Infrastructure (M365, Azure, GCP, Windows \& VDI) is responsible for the end\-to\-end delivery, operation, and performance of critical enterprise platforms, including Microsoft 365, Azure, GCP, Windows Server, VDI, and Citrix services.
   

  

 The role leads a team of approximately 15 engineers, ensuring the secure, stable, and efficient operation of business\-critical services, while delivering roadmap initiatives, platform improvements, and automation outcomes aligned to architecture and strategy.
   

  

 Working closely with Architecture, Security, Development, and wider Operations teams, the role ensures solutions are delivered, integrated, and operated at scale, with strong focus on service reliability, security, cost efficiency, and user experience.
   

  

 The role embeds operational excellence, resilience, automation, and governance, driving standardisation and continuous improvement across cloud, workplace, and identity platforms. Through strong leadership and execution, it reduces risk, complexity, and operational overhead, while improving service performance, stability, and cost control.
   

  

**What You Will Do**
 Leads engineering and operational delivery across M365, Azure, GCP, Windows, and VDI platforms, ensuring services, tooling, and integrations are
 **secure** 
 ,
 **resilient** 
 ,
 **scalable** 
 , and
 **operationally effective** 
 .
   

  

* Microsoft 365 Platforms: Ownership of collaboration and productivity services (Teams, Exchange, SharePoint, OneDrive, Intune), including service performance, governance, security, lifecycle, and user experience.
* Azure \& GCP Cloud: Operational management of cloud platforms including landing zones, subscriptions/projects, connectivity, platform services, and governance controls.
* Windows \& End User Platforms: Management of Windows Server and end\-user compute platforms, including build, patching, configuration, and lifecycle.
* VDI \& Citrix: Delivery and operation of virtual desktop environments, ensuring performance, availability, and user experience.
* Identity \& Access: Operational ownership of Active Directory and Entra ID, including authentication, Conditional Access, privileged access, and directory services.
* Cloud Security \& Compliance: Enforcement of secure configurations, monitoring, logging, and policy controls across all platforms.
* Leads delivery of standardisation, automation, tooling, and operational consistency across cloud and workplace platforms


 Key Accountabilities
   

  

**Service Delivery, Engineering \& Operations**
* Lead the build, delivery, and operation of critical infrastructure services across M365, cloud, Windows, and VDI
* Ensure platforms meet availability, performance, and service level expectations
* Act as senior escalation point for major incidents and service issues


**Security, Resilience \& Service Stability**
* Ensure secure\-by\-design and operational security controls are implemented and maintained
* Own resilience, recoverability, monitoring, and service continuity across platforms


**Roadmap Delivery \& Continuous Improvement**
* Deliver platform roadmaps and improvement initiatives in line with architectural direction
* Drive continuous improvement in automation, efficiency, and service quality


**Team Leadership \& Capability Development**
* Lead and develop a high\-performing engineering team (FTE \& contingent)
* Build capability across cloud, workplace, and identity domains
* Ensure effective workload management, prioritisation, and delivery


**Cross\-Domain Collaboration \& Integration**
* Work with Architecture, Security, Development, and Operations teams to deliver integrated services
* Ensure solutions are fully transitioned, supported, and operationally sustainable


**Vendor \& Service Management**
* Ensure service performance, delivery quality, and issue resolution


**Operational Governance, Cost \& Optimisation**
* Ensure services are aligned to standards and governed through full lifecycle
* Manage operational cost, capacity, and efficiency
* Drive automation, standardisation, and reduction of technical debt and complexity


 Leadership \& Behavioural Expectations
   

  

 Operates as a senior leader with strong ownership, accountability, and delivery focus.
   

  

* Ownership \& Accountability: Owns service outcomes across availability, security, and cost
* Leadership \& Team Development: Builds, leads, and develops high\-performing teams
* Execution \& Delivery: Drives consistent, high\-quality service and platform delivery
* Collaboration \& Influence: Works effectively across domains and stakeholders
* Adaptability \& Improvement: Responds to operational challenges while driving automation and optimisation


**Essential**
**What we’re looking for**
* Extensive experience leading infrastructure engineering and operations teams in enterprise environments
* Strong expertise across Microsoft 365 and workplace services, Azure and GCP cloud operations, Windows Server, VDI/Citrix, and identity (Active Directory, Entra), with robust knowledge of security, resilience, operational controls, and automation tooling.
* Proven ability to manage business\-critical services at scale
* Strong stakeholder, vendor, and team leadership capability
* Excellent operational, analytical, and decision\-making skills


**Soft Skills \& Behavioural Competencies**
* Ownership \& Accountability: Takes responsibility for delivering outcomes and driving technical excellence.
* Leadership \& Influence: Ability to inspire, motivate, and guide team members toward achieving goals.
* Communication: Clear and concise verbal and written communication skills for technical and non\-technical audiences.
* Problem\-Solving: Strong analytical skills to troubleshoot complex issues and implement effective solutions.
* Collaboration: Skilled at building relationships and working effectively across teams and stakeholders.
* Adaptability: Comfortable managing priorities in a fast\-paced, evolving environment.
* Continuous Improvement Mindset: Proactive in identifying opportunities for process and technology enhancements.


**Qualifications**
* Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or equivalent experience.
* Relevant certifications (e.g., Microsoft Certified: Azure Solutions Architect, Citrix Certified Expert, VMware VCP, Google Cloud Professional Architect) preferred.


**What We Offer You**
**Looking to make a mark?**
 At Colt, you’ll make a difference. Because around here, we empower people. We don’t tell you what to do.
   

  

 Instead, we employ people we trust, who come together across the globe to create intelligent solutions.
   

  

 Our global teams are full of ambitious, driven people, all working together towards one shared purpose: to put the power of the digital universe in the hands of our customers wherever, whenever and however they want.
   

  

 We give our people the opportunity to inspire and lead teams, and work on projects that connect people, cities, businesses, and ideas. We want you to help us change the world, for the better.
   

  

**Diversity and inclusion**
* Inclusion and valuing diversity of thought and experience are at the heart of our culture here at Colt. From day one, you’ll be encouraged to be yourself because we believe that’s what helps our people to thrive. We welcome people with diverse backgrounds and experiences, regardless of their gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, race, religion, disability, neurodiversity, age, marital status, pregnancy status, or place of birth.


**Most Recently We Have**
* Signed the UN Women Empowerment Principles which guide our Gender Action Plan
* Trained 60 (and growing) Colties to be Mental Health First Aiders
* Please speak with a member of our recruitment team if you require adjustments to our recruitment process to support you. For more information about our Inclusion and Diversity agenda, visit our DEI pages.


**Benefits**
 Our benefits support you through all parts of life, for both physical and mental health.
   

  

* Flexible working hours and the option to work from home.
* Extensive induction program with experienced mentors and buddies.
* Opportunities for further development and educational opportunities.
* Global Family Leave Policy.
* Employee Assistance Program.
* Internal inclusion \& diversity employee networks.


**A global network**
* When you join Colt you become part of our global network. We are proud of our colleagues and the stories and experience they bring – take a look at ‘Our People’ site including our Empowered Women in Tech.

e2c68fbe.title: Pre-Sales Manager-Channel
e2c68fbe.location: Sahibzada Ajit Singh Nagar, Punjab, India
e2c68fbe.description:
**Job Summary**
 We are seeking an experienced Pre\-Sales Manager to lead solution design and technical sales support for our IT infrastructure and last\-mile/field support services. The role will work closely with sales and partner teams to create compelling, technically sound solutions that address customer requirements across enterprise IT environments.
   

  

 The ideal candidate has strong hands\-on experience in IT infrastructure, field support operations, and customer\-facing pre\-sales engagements, with the ability to translate business needs into scalable technical solutions.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Own the pre\-sales lifecycle for IT infrastructure, last\-mile support, and field services opportunities.
* Work closely with sales teams to understand customer requirements, pain points, and business objectives.
* Prepare technical proposals, RFP/RFI responses, SoWs, and solution presentations.
* Conduct customer workshops, technical discussions, and solution walkthroughs.
* Define service models, SLAs, KPIs, and support frameworks aligned with customer expectations.
* Support pricing, costing, and effort estimation in coordination with delivery and finance teams.
* Stay updated on industry trends, tools, and best practices in IT infrastructure and field support services.
* Provide pre\-sales leadership and mentoring to junior team members as needed.
* Design end\-to\-end solutions covering desktop \& end\-user computing support, onsite/field support services, network, server, storage, and data center infrastructure, and IT asset lifecycle management and IMAC services.


**Required Skills \& Experience**
* Strong experience in IT infrastructure pre\-sales within an IT services organization.
* Proven experience handling enterprise customers and large\-scale IT services deals.
* Excellent customer\-facing, presentation, and communication skills.
* Ability to translate technical concepts into business value.
* Experience with RFPs, solution design, effort estimation, and pricing models.
* Strong stakeholder management , analytical, and collaboration skills.
* Hands\-on understanding of field/last\-mile IT support operations/end\-user computing (L1–L3 support)/network, server, storage, and data center environments and ITSM frameworks (ITIL)


**Preferred Qualifications**
* ITIL certification (Foundation or higher)
* Infrastructure certifications (Microsoft, Cisco, VMware, etc.)
* Experience with multi\-location or geographically distributed field support models.
* Exposure to global delivery and managed services environments.


**What We Offer**
* Opportunity to work on large\-scale IT infrastructure and field support engagements.
* High\-visibility role with direct impact on business growth.
* Collaborative work environment with strong sales and delivery teams.

60c8ce51.title: Delivery Manager I
60c8ce51.description:
**Role Description**
**Job Title:**
**Senior Technical Project Manager \- Enterprise Applications (Offshore Delivery)**
**Location:**
**India (Trivandrum / Chennai / Bangalore / Hyderabad)**
**Experience**
**15\-20\+ Years**
**Job Description**
**We are looking for strong Technical Project Managers to lead offshore execution for large\-scale enterprise programs aligned to architecture\-driven initiatives (.NET and Node/React \+ Gen AI platforms).**
**This role requires end\-to\-end delivery ownership, strong technical understanding, and the ability to drive programs from red to green, ensuring timelines, quality, and customer satisfaction.**
**Key Responsibilities**
* Own end\-to\-end delivery execution from offshore for large enterprise programs
* Understand functional and technical requirements and translate into execution plans
* Ensure zero / minimal milestone slippage through proactive tracking and governance
* Drive accelerated delivery leveraging AI tools and accelerators
* Identify risks early and implement strong risk mitigation strategies
* Lead program governance, status reporting, and milestone tracking
* Manage cross\-functional teams (engineering, QA, DevOps)
* Drive legacy modernization / migration programs (critical requirement)
* Partner with architects to ensure design\-to\-delivery alignment
* Work closely with onshore stakeholders and customers
* Ensure proactive communication, escalations, and resolution management


**Mandatory Skills**
* Strong experience in:
+ Technical project/program management (15\+ years)
+ Managing large enterprise application delivery

* Good technical understanding of:
+ .NET / Node / Full Stack architectures
+ Microservices, APIs, and modern architecture concepts

* Expertise in:
+ Schedule management \& milestone tracking
+ Risk management \& mitigation
+ Stakeholder communication \& governance

* Experience in:
+ Legacy system modernization / migration programs
+ Agile / SAFe delivery models

* Strong experience in:
+ Driving recovery programs (red green execution)


**Good to Have**
* Exposure to AI\-driven delivery accelerators / productivity tools
* Experience in US Healthcare domain
* Experience working with distributed global teams


**Soft Skills (Critical)**
* Strong communication and presentation skills
* Proactive mindset with high ownership
* Ability to handle ambiguity and drive clarity
* Strong customer collaboration and stakeholder management
* Flexible and execution\-focused
* Ability to influence teams and leadership


**Role Expectations**
* Act as the single point of accountability for offshore delivery
* Ensure tight coordination with onshore architects and stakeholders
* Drive daily/weekly governance with clear transparency
* Focus on predictability, quality, and accelerated timelines

 delivery management,healthcare domain,software modernization,us healthcare,program management,

ab72e5a0.title: Delivery Lead
ab72e5a0.description: Delivery Leads manage the end\-to\-end delivery of projects and ensure successful outcomes. Responsibilities include overseeing project execution, managing resources, and ensuring alignment with client requirements. You will work with senior management to drive project success and address complex challenges. Extensive experience in project management and strong leadership abilities are required.

2d4528ca.title: Google Workspace Delivery Manager, Google Cloud
2d4528ca.description:
Note: By applying to this position you will have an opportunity to share your preferred working location from the following:
 **Bengaluru, Karnataka, India; Hyderabad, Telangana, India** 
 .
 **Minimum qualifications:**
* Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Mathematics, a related technical field, or equivalent practical experience.
* 8 years of experience managing cross\-functional technical teams, stakeholders, and external deployment partners.
* Experience programming in one or more of the following: Java, Python, Go, C\+\+, or similar programming languages.
* Experience with cloud computing, IT systems integration, and deploying enterprise SaaS solutions (specifically Google Workspace).


**Preferred qualifications:**
* Professional certification in Google Workspace Administration or Google Cloud.
* Good understanding of the Workspace or equivalent products and a clear, business\-oriented grasp of its principles and potential. Demonstrated ability to communicate complex technical concepts as business value and ROI to executive stakeholders.
* Demonstrated record in implementing large\-scale cloud or software projects in corporate environments.
* Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the ability to clearly explain technical tradeoffs to both executive stakeholders and engineering teams.


**About The Job**
 The Google Cloud Platform team helps customers transform and build what's next for their business — all with technology built in the cloud. Our products are developed for security, reliability and scalability, running the full stack from infrastructure to applications to devices and hardware. Our teams are dedicated to helping our customers — developers, small and large businesses, educational institutions and government agencies — see the benefits of our technology come to life. As part of an entrepreneurial team in this rapidly growing business, you will play a key role in understanding the needs of our customers and help shape the future of businesses of all sizes use technology to connect with customers, employees and partners.
   

  

 As a Delivery Manager in Google Cloud’s Professional Services organization, you will lead a team of technical consultants and Cloud engineers providing consulting services to enterprise organizations adopting Google Workspace and associated solutions. You will directly manage client engagements for your team, interfacing with executive client and internal stakeholders to ensure services are delivered to schedule, to margin goals, and with positive customer feedback. You will also provide leadership on scoping potential services and developing a business roadmap for professional services offerings, ensuring alignment with Sales and Product priorities.
   

  

 Google Cloud accelerates every organization’s ability to digitally transform its business and industry. We deliver enterprise\-grade solutions that leverage Google’s cutting\-edge technology, and tools that help developers build more sustainably. Customers in more than 200 countries and territories turn to Google Cloud as their trusted partner to enable growth and solve their most critical business problems.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Manage the end\-to\-end delivery and technical strategy for Google Workspace deployments, ensuring architectural alignment and smooth data migrations from legacy systems. Act as the central interface, coordinating complex technical requirements between customers, internal cross\-functional teams, and external system integrators.
* Define project scope, establish baselines, and manage schedules while identifying and mitigating risks to successfully deliver projects on time and within budget. Conduct regular service reviews, monitor Service Level Agreement (SLA), and track quality metrics to ensure optimal service delivery and customer satisfaction.
* Guide customers through technical change management and user adoption strategies to maximize the value of their Google Workspace investment.
* Consult clients on best practices, security protocols, and the rollout of new Workspace features, including Generative AI integrations.
* Provide technical implementation support in customer environments, including guidance on implementation feasibility of cross\-product integrations.


 Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

8f525004.title: Program Manager
8f525004.location: Sahibzada Ajit Singh Nagar, Punjab, India
8f525004.description:
**Company Overview**
 Iron Systems is an innovative, customer\-focused provider of custom\-built computing infrastructure platforms such as network servers, storage, OEM/ODM appliances \& embedded systems. For more than 15 years, customers have trusted us for our innovative problem solving combined with holistic design, engineering, manufacturing, logistics, and global support services.
   

  

**Experience Required: 6\-10 years.**
**Job Description**
* End\-to\-end Project Planning and Coordination (Activities and Resources)
* Responsible for overall project processes and deliverables
* Project onboarding, daily tracking of performance, proactive communication and escalation management
* Drive the highest level of customer satisfaction and be a customer\-referenceable account.
* Issue identification, resolution and stakeholder management
* Project kick\-off, weekly governance, documentation, reports and dashboards
* Partnership with customers and stakeholders with a customer\-focused mindset
* Process and Service Improvement and Identification of new business opportunities
* BGV, Compliance, training and onboarding as per customer process
* Timely escalation to internal teams to take necessary actions.
* Timely invoicing, collection of payments, and billing data to be sent on the 1st working day of the month to the customer.
* Daily status reporting to SDM
* Quality control, knowledge management, and best\-practice sharing.
* IT Operations and Service Operation
* Handling Change Requests in Project Management
* Maintain Project documentation and course folders, SLA, PMS


**Skillset**
* Bachelor of Technology in Computer Science or related field
* Information Technology Infrastructure Library (ITIL) Certified
* PMP or PRINCE2 certified (good to have)

ec280d49.title: Program Manager - Enterprise SMB
ec280d49.description:
**We are hiring \| Program Manager – Jio Enterprise SMB** 




  





 Jio Enterprise SMB is looking for high\-potential
 **Program Managers** 
 to help build and scale new businesses across multiple verticals.
 



  





 This is not a conventional strategy role. You will work on businesses that are being built from the ground up—shaping the operating plan, defining go\-to\-market approach, creating channel programs, and driving execution with cross\-functional teams.
 



  





**What you will work on:** 




 • Co\-owning annual operating plans and growth initiatives
 



 • Designing and executing segment × channel GTM strategies
 



 • Building channel programs and ensuring on\-ground adoption
 



 • Creating processes, systems, and operating rhythms for scale
 



 • Driving business outcomes, including revenue and P\&L\-linked priorities, for identified segments/verticals
 



 We are looking for professionals with
 **4–6 years of experience** 
 in consulting, business strategy, growth, category management, or high\-impact program management roles.
 



  





**Preferred profile:** 




 • Top\-tier MBA with a strong engineering/technical undergraduate background
 



 • Strong problem\-solving and structured\-thinking capability
 



 • Ability to move from strategy to execution and own outcomes
 



 • Comfortable working in an entrepreneurial, fast\-paced environment
 



 • Strong stakeholder\-management and communication skills
 



  





 This is an opportunity to work on meaningful business problems at scale, while having real ownership of both the decisions and the outcomes.
 



  





 Interested candidates may share their profile with Arti.Kondal@ril.com

951303e4.title: Associate Program Manager
951303e4.description:
At Pocket, we’ve experienced rapid growth in both revenue and scale over the past few years, further fueling our ambition. We operate across diverse industries and geographies, tackling complex challenges that require leaders who can navigate ambiguity and drive impact. These roles demand the ability to solve first\-of\-their\-kind problems, often without precedent.
 



  





 This position involves leading key functions, including creative teams, production, digital marketing, and user acquisition; as well as managing large\-scale, cross\-functional initiatives. Thriving at Pocket requires a mix of high IQ and EQ, analytical rigor, an entrepreneurial mindset, and the ability to influence C\-suite decision\-making. We set ambitious goals, solve complex problems, and take full ownership of execution. Prior experience solving similar problems isn’t required. What matters is adaptability, a willingness to learn and reinvent, and intellectual humility.
 



  





**Responsibilities** 




  





 This role sits at the intersection of content strategy, product thinking, and operational excellence solving first\-time, high\-scale challenges across a rapidly expanding content ecosystem.
 



  




* **Content Library Ownership:** 
 End\-to\-end ownership of the licensed content library, ensuring quality, consistency, and high\-impact content availability across genres.
* Run structured experiments, analyze performance signals, and identify top\-performing genres.
* **Operational Program Management:** 
 Build programs and workflows that guarantee a steady, timely flow of content going live fully aligned with growth targets and business goals.
* **Team \& Talent Leadership:** 
 Hire, scale, and lead editorial and promo production teams. Drive talent development through structured training, workshops, incentives, and ongoing capability\-building.
* **Data\-Driven Content Decisions:** 
 Track show performance, identify improvement levers, and shape the future pipeline by pairing editorial intuition with analytical rigor.
* **Process Innovation:** 
 Design and refine end\-to\-end processes for creation, distribution, and repurposing. Keep teams aligned on product innovations and integrate new features seamlessly into the content strategy.



  





**Qualifications** 




  




* Minimum 6 months to 1 year of work experience in a startup/similar environment.
* Astute problem\-solving skills and ability to break (seemingly) large problems into smaller and practical solvable pieces, and a data\-driven approach to decision\-making, with a keen eye for insights and metrics
* Passionate about building great product experiences, thriving in fast\-moving environments, and making smart decisions in ambiguity.

d8d61655.title: Global Engagement Manager
d8d61655.description:
**PACCAR India** 
 is seeking an experienced
 **Global Engagement Manager** 
 who will be responsible for overseeing strategic IT initiatives, projects, and programs across various divisions. The role has overall responsibility to manage and provide governance for projects delivered by PACCAR India teams and partner (supplier) teams, while ensuring strong delivery performance, stakeholder engagement, team effectiveness, and expansion of IT capabilities from India. The position acts as a single owner for all projects and sustainment work delivered from India for assigned division(s), working closely with Supplier Delivery teams and Division Application Managers.This position will be responsible for managing the team that is responsible for PIPL’s project and program deliveries for various divisions and will ensure the department’s efficiency and effectiveness in collaboration. Report into PACCAR India IT Director for overall success of delivery from India.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Delivery Management:
* Tracking \& Status reporting of key programs
* Manage escalations
* Facilitating managements updates
* Develop strong supplier relationship


* 
+ Develop and execute the PACCAR IT strategy aligned with business goals and objectives.
+ Stay updated on emerging technologies and industry trends and assess their potential for implementation to enhance operational efficiency.
+ Ensure compliance with the security practices and no violations are reported on the team for security, privacy or audit findings
+ Participate in evaluation of software applications and tools that align with business needs, including ERP systems, productivity software, and collaboration tools from IT Delivery perspective. Further contribute to the implementation from Infrastructure standpoint
+ Maintain thorough documentation of IT project status and generate reports on team performance
+ Stakeholder Management:
+ Act as liaison between India delivery teams, suppliers, and Division Application Managers
+ Drive regular engagement with global stakeholders across locations
+ Improve communication, transparency, and collaboration with stakeholders
+ Build and maintain strong relationships with division stakeholders and suppliers
+ Ensure supplier performance meets defined expectations, including high CSAT (target ≥9\.1\)
+ Work with division stakeholders to define and execute resource growth and capacity plans
+ Collaborate with division leadership on strategic India delivery alignment and expansion
+ Negotiate and manage SLAs, contracts, and service delivery commitments
+ Ensure effective governance across supplier and internal delivery teams
+ Team Management:
+ Drive team performance through goal setting, performance reviews, and continuous feedback
+ Enable skill development through structured learning initiatives, with focus on significant training hours
+ Improve team motivation, engagement, and overall productivity
+ Maintain team stability by managing attrition in line with industry benchmarks
+ Foster a culture of accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement
+ Identify skill gaps and build capability through targeted development plans
+ Manage team’s shifts , overtime , non standard working
+ Onboarding of new position \& transition
+ Identify new opportunities and collaborate with stakeholders to grow IT delivery from India
+ Work with recruitment teams to ensure smooth onboarding of new resources
+ Participate in evaluating technical requirements, shortlisting candidates, and supporting hiring decisions
+ Collaborate with stakeholders to identify transition opportunities and resource movement
+ Work with suppliers and HR teams to ensure seamless transition and knowledge transfer
+ Drive expansion of India IT team to support business growth, including planned increase in headcount
+ Ensure effective onboarding, integration, and productivity of new hires
+ Finance Management
+ Ensure on time submission of invoicing \& effort details with controller
+ Ensure projection update for every month to the controller
+ Ensure compilation \& validation of teams nonstandard working hours \& then submit it to HR for payout


* Other projects or tasks as assigned
+ Application Security review – work with division security team to validate security compliance of any new application or platform which is planned for PACCAR India
+ Support PACCAR Internship program – identification of project, shortlisting \& interview of candidate \& onboarding
+ Onboarding \& Mentoring the interns throughout their journey


**Education Requirements**
* Bachelor’s or Master’s Degree in Computer Science or a related field.


**Knowledge \& Skills REQUIRED**
* Minimum 15 years of relevant IT experience
* Minimum of 8 – 10 years as an IT Project Manager/Program Manager or similar position with complete responsibility.
* Strong technical knowledge of IT Project and Program Management, including project planning, stakeholder management and cross location deliveries.
* Depth on cross border collaboration and interaction with senior level IT/Business teams, relationship management is a must


**Competencies And Behaviours**
* Strong understanding of formal project management methodologies.
* Excellent leadership and management skills, with the ability to motivate and develop a team. Ability to lead project teams of various sizes and see them through to completion.
* Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to build relationships with colleagues and stakeholders.
* Analytical and problem\-solving skills, with the ability to identify and resolve issues quickly.
* Knowledge of relevant regulations and compliance requirements.
* Ability to manage budgets and forecast expenditures.
* Ability to adapt to new technologies and industry trends.
* Knowledge of relevant compliance requirements
* Display effective and professional communication skills (verbal and written) including interpersonal skills.
* Interact effectively and in a professional manner with all contacts including external and internal customers and with suppliers .
* Able to work in a team environment.
* Coordinate multiple concurrent tasks effectively

b2c3602c.title: Program Manager - Precision and Vehicle Controls
b2c3602c.description:
**Requisition ID:** 4950
  
**Job Location:** Gurgaon \- India
  
**Category:** Research and Development
  
**Job Type:** Full Time
  
**Working Mode:** Fully On\-Site
  
**\#body.unify div.unify\-button\-container .unify\-apply\-now:** focus, \#body.unify div.unify\-button\-container .unify\-apply\-\#body.unify div.unify\-button\-container .unify\-apply\-now: focus, \#body.unify div.unify\-button\-container .unify\-apply\-applying with LinkedIn
  
  

About Us
  
  

Innovation. Sustainability. Productivity. This is how we are Breaking New Ground in our mission to sustainably advance the noble work of farmers and builders everywhere. With a growing global population and increased demands on resources, our products are instrumental to feeding and sheltering the world.
  
  

From developing products that run on alternative power to productivity\-enhancing precision tech, we are delivering solutions that benefit people – and they are possible thanks to people like you. If the opportunity to build your skills as part of a collaborative, global team excites you, you’re in the right place.
  
  

Grow a Career. Build a Future!
  
  

Be part of this company at the forefront of agriculture and construction, that passionately innovates to drive customer efficiency and success. And we know innovation can’t happen without collaboration. So, everything we do at CNH Industrial is about reaching new heights as one team, always delivering for the good of our customers.
  
  

Precision and Vehicle Controls Program Manager
  
  

The Program Manager is responsible for overseeing and coordinating multiple projects within an organization, ensuring they align with the company’s strategic objectives. This role involves managing project teams, resources, and timelines, ensuring that programs are delivered on time, within scope, and within budget.
  
  

As the Precision Electronics Program Manager with Vehicle Electronics \& Precision Applications team, you will be responsible to plan, manage, and direct all development activities of Multiple programs, to meet customer needs and fit within the capabilities of the Precision Electronics team. The team works across component and platform programs to ensure that on time launch of products to meet the customer needs, thereby having a positive impact on the business
  
  

Key Responsibilities
  
Manage end to end execution of Precision Electronics deliverables for platform integration programs
  
Ensure the programs meet their quality / delivery (timing) / scope / financial objectives
  
Work closely with internal and external teams (Business platform, Technical Product Managers, Engineering/Development, Architecture, Security, Product Validation, quality etc.) to develop and execute short\-term and long\-term plans, policies, procedures, resources, and initiatives.
  
Work with leadership and program teams to maintain accountability to budgets, plans, and initiatives.
  
Gain knowledge of the customer and business needs; thereby contributing to strategic decisions.
  
Measure and monitor project performance; Create, maintain and review project status reports to support close surveillance over Solutions development and support events to assure proper allocation of resources.
  
Ensure project plans and timelines are met by internal and external resources through Program Increment (PI) team plans and commitments.
  
Understand and manage the program risk throughout the life cycle; initiate mitigations, or corrective actions as necessary.
  
Serve as a liaison and mentor to assist with and expedite product related issues.
  
Monitor and initiate improvement projects to current processes.
  
Holds post\-program lessons learned to look for issues and opportunities for improvement.
  
Effectively communicate to stakeholders with the appropriate content and frequency.
  
Provide feedback to leadership regarding process items that could improve the efficiency and effectiveness of the entire product development process.
  
  

Experience Required
  
Minimum of 8 years of experience in software/electronic hardware development programs; or equivalent combination of education and experience
  
Knowledge of product development and procedures, including general finance exposure; both Waterfall Methodology and Agile Framework \- such as SCRUM, SAFe
  
Experience managing budgets and increasing efficiencies
  
PMP or Six Sigma preferred
  
Demonstrated leadership, organizational, documentation, analytical, and decision\-making skills
  
Strong skills in the Microsoft Office Suite (Outlook, Excel, Project) and Tools like Polarion, JIRA, Confluence
  
Ability to understand and drive various Root Cause Analysis techniques in Embedded Software Domain
  
  

.
  
  

Preferred Qualifications
  
Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, business, or related technical field
  
What We Offer
  
  

We offer dynamic career opportunities across an international landscape. As an equal opportunity employer, we are committed to delivering value for all our employees and fostering a culture of respect.
  
  

At CNH, we understand that the best solutions come from the diverse experiences and skills of our people. Here, you will be empowered to grow your career, to follow your passion, and help build a better future. To support our employees, we offer regional comprehensive benefits, including:
  
  

Flexible work arrangements
  
Savings \& Retirement benefits
  
Tuition reimbursement
  
Parental leave
  
Adoption assistance
  
Fertility \& Family building support
  
Employee Assistance Programs
  
Charitable contribution matching and Volunteer Time Off
  
**\#body.unify div.unify\-button\-container .unify\-apply\-now:** focus, \#body.unify div.unify\-button\-container .unify\-apply\-\#body.unify div.unify\-button\-container .unify\-apply\-now: focus, \#body.unify div.unify\-button\-container .unify\-apply\-applying with LinkedIn

51a2b094.title: Advanced Specialist, Program Manager
51a2b094.description:
**The opportunity**

Pearson is transforming how 160 million learners and thousands of employees experience AI — and we need someone to make that change land with people, not just on systems. This role leads enterprise AI change management for Pearson, owning the communities, content, and communications that turn our AI strategy into everyday action across every business unit.


Reporting directly to the AI Strategy \& Governance lead in the AI Center for Enablement, you will be the S\&G function's authority on change management and AI literacy — working through influence across HR, Corporate communications, Brand, and Business Units.


**What you'll do**

* **Lead change.** Design and own Pearson's enterprise AI change management strategy — stakeholder analysis, readiness planning, and execution for major AI rollouts, governance changes, and tool adoptions.
* **Own the community.** Build and run the AI Adoption Partners Programme — Pearson's embedded AI ambassador network — from role design and onboarding through curriculum, community management, and outcomes measurement (targeting 80% of partners reporting they feel equipped).
* **Build the content.** Create persona\-based AI literacy resources — guides, toolkits, playbooks, and training — that make responsible AI accessible and motivating for every employee level and function.
* **Shape the narrative.** Craft and coordinate the internal AI change communications calendar alongside Brand/CMC, translating complex AI strategy into clear, compelling narratives using Smart Brevity principles — ensuring messaging is concise, actionable, and easy to apply for executives, managers, and frontline staff.
* **Measure what matters.** Track adoption health through KPI dashboards and surveys; synthesise insights; and report programme performance to senior leadership and the AI Strategy Council.
* **Work across the enterprise.** Coordinate with HR, L\&D, Legal, and Business Unit leaders to align the adoption programme to Pearson's people strategy and ensure it scales across geographies.

**What you'll bring**

* **Enterprise change ownership.** You have designed, launched, and measured change programmes at scale in a large, complex organisation — you have owned programmes, not just supported them.
* **Methodology fluency.** You know a structured change framework deeply — PROSCI ADKAR, Kotter, APMG, or equivalent — and can adapt it to AI and digital transformation contexts.
* **Community\-building.** You have built and sustained ambassador networks, change champion cohorts, or adoption programmes that become more self\-sufficient over time.
* **Communication excellence.** You translate technical or strategic complexity into clear, high\-impact narratives that drive action across all audiences. You apply Smart Brevity to cut through noise while maintaining accuracy, and you demonstrate exceptional attention to detail in everything you produce.
* **Precision and standards.** You hold a high bar for quality — producing communications that are concise, consistent, and error\-free, with strong attention to detail and alignment to brand and governance standards.
* **Cross\-functional influence.** You have coordinated across HR, L\&D, Communications, and Business Units in a matrixed environment, building alignment without formal authority.
* **AI literacy.** You understand AI well enough to design credible content — conversant in AI tools, responsible use principles, and what good and poor AI adoption looks like in practice.

**It would be great if you also have**

* PROSCI Certification (CCMP) or equivalent — APMG Change Management Practitioner, ACMP CCMP.
* Hands\-on experience with enterprise AI tools (Microsoft 365 Copilot, GitHub Copilot) or familiarity with AI governance frameworks (Responsible AI, NIST AI RMF).
* Experience designing digital or blended learning content, or working with LMS platforms.
* A background in education, EdTech, or another purpose\-driven, learner\-centred sector

**Who we are:**

At Pearson, our purpose is simple: to help people realize the life they imagine through learning. We believe that every learning opportunity is a chance for a personal breakthrough. We are the world's lifelong learning company. For us, learning isn't just what we do. It's who we are. To learn more: We are Pearson.


Pearson is an Equal Opportunity Employer and a member of E\-Verify. Employment decisions are based on qualifications, merit and business need. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, ethnicity, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, national origin, protected veteran status, disability status or any other group protected by law. We actively seek qualified candidates who are protected veterans and individuals with disabilities as defined under VEVRAA and Section 503 of the Rehabilitation Act.


If you are an individual with a disability and are unable or limited in your ability to use or access our career site as a result of your disability, you may request reasonable accommodations by emailing TalentExperienceGlobalTeam@grp.pearson.com.
